title: Introduction
description: NOC 64314 – Hotel front desk clerks | GTR Immigration As the primary point of contact in Canada's multi-billion dollar hospitality industry, NOC 64314 (Hotel front desk clerks) are essential to the nation's tourism brand. These TEER 4 professionals manage the first impressions of international travelers, handling everything from reservations to guest concierge services. This role is a highly popular entry point for immigration, offering specialized hospitality-focused pathways in provinces like British Columbia and Alberta.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level4Major Group64 – Sales and service representativesSub-Major Group643 – Occupations in food, accommodation and tourismMinor Group6431 – Occupations in travel and accommodationNOC Code64314

duties: Greet guests and assign rooms according to reservations and availability.
Maintain records of room availability and guest accounts using property management systems.
Handle guest check-outs, process payments, and provide information on hotel services.
Address guest complaints and coordinate with housekeeping or maintenance staff.
Provide concierge-style information on local attractions, transportation, and dining.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
Completion of a college program in hotel management or a related field is often preferred for luxury establishments.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. Projections indicate over 20,000 job openings nationally as Canada expands its tourism infrastructure and hotel capacity.
labour_market_demand: The demand for front desk clerks is Good to Very Good. With Canada hosting major international events through 2026, hotels and resorts are facing a critical staff shortage. Multi-lingual candidates are particularly sought after by luxury hotels and resorts in urban and mountain destinations.
Hospitality Career Ladder: Direct progression to Accommodation Service Manager (NOC 60031).
Inclusive Environments: Work in diverse, multicultural teams with world-class service training.
Seasonal Flexibility: Opportunities to work in scenic mountain resorts (Whistler/Banff) or bustling city centers.
pathways: BC PNP Entry-Level and Semi-Skilled: A dedicated stream for hospitality workers.
Alberta Opportunity Stream: Fast-track PR for those currently working in Alberta's hotels.
CEC (Express Entry): Accessible after one year of Canadian hospitality experience.
